solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean detectCapitalUse(String word) {
        int capitalCount = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) {
            if (Character.isUpperCase(word.charAt(i))) {
                capitalCount++;
            }
        }
        return capitalCount == 0 || capitalCount == word.length() || (capitalCount == 1 && Character.isUpperCase(word.charAt(0)));
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution counts the number of uppercase letters in the string and checks if it meets one of the valid conditions: all lowercase, all uppercase, or only the first letter uppercase. It is efficient with linear time complexity and constant space usage.
